channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Prospects fade for imminent end to Iran war as attacks restart
item.description: Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.
item.content:encoded:
Israel says it will intensify attacks against Hezbollah in Lebanon and U.S. military struck Iranian boats and missile launch sites as envoys continued negotiations for a deal that would end the three-month war.

(Image credit: AFP via Getty Images)
item.dc:creator: Jane Arraf

item.title: U.S. strikes Iran. And, immigration courts use new tactic to speed up deportations
item.description: The U.S. military has launched new attacks on Iran while talks to end the conflict are ongoing. And, the Department of Justice is using a new tactic in immigration courts to accelerate deportations.
item.content:encoded:
The U.S. military has launched new attacks on Iran while talks to end the conflict are ongoing. And, the Department of Justice is using a new tactic in immigration courts to accelerate deportations.

(Image credit: Michael M. Santiago)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: Texas GOP voters vote in race that could shape future of the party -- and the Senate
item.description: Controversial Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is challenging U.S. Sen. John Cornyn's reelection. The $100 million fight could have far-reaching implications for the GOP, and party control of the Senate.
item.content:encoded:
Controversial Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton is challenging U.S. Sen. John Cornyn's reelection. The $100 million fight could have far-reaching implications for the GOP, and party control of the Senate.

(Image credit: Julio Cortez)
item.dc:creator: Claudia Grisales

item.title: Therapists are using AI to take notes. Is it a useful tool or a breach of trust?
item.description: New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.
item.content:encoded:
New companies are selling artificial intelligence assistance to mental health therapists. The AI tools can help with administration and recordkeeping, but some patients worry about their privacy.

(Image credit: Fiordaliso)
item.dc:creator: Windsor Johnston

item.title: In West Texas, an unlikely alliance stands against extending the border wall
item.description: An unusual coalition of people across the political spectrum have banded together to rally against a border wall in the Big Bend.
item.content:encoded:
An unusual coalition of people across the political spectrum have banded together to rally against a border wall in the Big Bend.

(Image credit: Carlos Morales)
item.dc:creator: Carlos Morales

item.title: Immigration courts are using a new tactic to speed up deportations
item.description: The Justice Department is moving up the court hearings for hundreds of immigrants and scheduling them for mass hearings. If they don't show up, they could be ordered deported.
item.content:encoded:
The Justice Department is moving up the court hearings for hundreds of immigrants and scheduling them for mass hearings. If they don't show up, they could be ordered deported.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo

item.title: Inside ATL: how Delta juggles 100,000 bags a day at the world's busiest airport
item.description: On a busy day, Delta Air Lines handles more than 100,000 bags at its Atlanta hub. NPR got a rare look behind the scenes at how the airline is using AI to improve baggage-handling operations.
item.content:encoded:
On a busy day, Delta Air Lines handles more than 100,000 bags at its Atlanta hub. NPR got a rare look behind the scenes at how the airline is using AI to improve baggage-handling operations.

(Image credit: Alyssa Pointer for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: U.S. military says it struck Iran Monday in "self-defense," Russia threatens more strikes against Ukraine, Texas GOP voters head to polls for primary that could shape future of the party.
item.content:encoded: U.S. military says it struck Iran Monday in "self-defense," Russia threatens more strikes against Ukraine, Texas GOP voters head to polls for primary that could shape future of the party.
item.dc:creator: Leila Fadel

item.title: Attacks from residents complicate the fight against a rare type of Ebola
item.description: Three times in the past week, healthcare facilities have been attacked. On Sunday, angry young men stormed a hospital treating Ebola patients, forcing medical staff to evacuate them as gunfire rang out.
item.content:encoded:
Three times in the past week, healthcare facilities have been attacked. On Sunday, angry young men stormed a hospital treating Ebola patients, forcing medical staff to evacuate them as gunfire rang out.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: New York back in NBA Finals for first time since 1999 after beating Cleveland
item.description: New York will play the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder or San Antonio Spurs in the finals. The Western Conference finals is tied at two games apiece with Game 5 to be played on Tuesday.
item.content:encoded:
New York will play the defending champion Oklahoma City Thunder or San Antonio Spurs in the finals. The Western Conference finals is tied at two games apiece with Game 5 to be played on Tuesday.

(Image credit: Sue Ogrocki)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
